title: APM Spec Guardian - Panelist Return Shape (advisory regime)
description: Shape every spec-review panelist MUST return when invoked by the apm-spec-guardian skill. The panel runs over the OpenAPM specification artifact; each finding carries a severity bucket (blocking / recommended / nit) and the synthesizer arbitrates. There is NO binary verdict; the orchestrator does not compute APPROVE/REJECT. The shocked_meter scale (1..10) is the panelist's overall caliber rating of the artifact post-edit.

persona.description: Panelist slug. MUST equal the .agent.md filename stem.

round.description: Round number this return was produced in. Round 1 is the first pass; round 2+ only in new-version mode (where the drafter has produced an updated SPEC_DRAFT in response to round-1 findings).

overall_stance.description: Panelist's overall stance. Advisory only; the synthesizer aggregates and decides.

round_1_blocker_closure.description: Required field; MUST be an empty array in round 1. In round 2+, one entry per round-1 blocker raised BY THIS PANELIST, reporting whether and how the drafter closed it.

id.description: The blocker id this panelist assigned in round 1 (e.g. sw-blk-1).

evidence.description: What changed in the artifact (with section / line cite if possible).

remaining_concern.description: Null if status is closed; otherwise the residual concern.

round_1_recommended_closure.description: Narrative one-liner summarizing round-1 recommended-finding closure rate. Empty string in round 1.

new_blocking_findings.description: Findings the panelist asserts would break a conformant implementation, leak a security guarantee, or invalidate a published normative claim. Use sparingly. The synthesizer's blocker-veto rule triggers if the sum across all panels is > 0.

new_recommended_findings.description: Substantive improvements (clarifications, missing edge cases, hardening of a defensive MUST). The default bucket for non-trivial feedback.

new_nit_findings.description: One-line editorial polish (typos, stale heading labels, count-drift). Each entry MAY omit recommended_fix if the fix is obvious from the finding text.

regressions_in_praised_strengths.description: Round-2+ only. Empty in round 1. Each entry names a strength the panelist praised in round 1 that has since regressed in the new draft.

preserved_strengths_confirmed.description: Strengths of the artifact the panelist explicitly confirms remain intact. Used by the synthesizer to detect regressions and by the comment template's <details> tail.

shocked_meter.description: Overall caliber rating of the artifact in its current state. 1 = unshippable; 7 = ship-quality with followups; 10 = best-in-class standards-body caliber. Calibration: a typical mature OSS spec at v1.0 lands around 7..8; OpenAPM v0.1 round-2 landed at 8 across all four panels.

summary.description: One to three sentences. ASCII only. Suitable for the per-panel row in the convergence table.

confidence.description: Panelist's confidence in their own assessment. low = unfamiliar territory; high = core competence.

id.description: Stable id of the form <persona-prefix>-<bucket>-r<round>-<index>, e.g. sw-rec-r2-1 or oci-blk-r1-3.

section_ref.description: Section / appendix / req-XXX anchor reference (e.g. 'sec.12.3' or 'req-mf-019' or 'Appendix A manifest schema').

finding.description: Concrete problem statement, ASCII only. Should be self-contained enough that the synthesizer can cluster it without rereading the artifact.

recommended_fix.description: Concrete patch instruction, ASCII only. SHOULD be small enough to apply mechanically. If the fix would require adding a vendor / foundation token (CNCF, Linux Foundation, Sandbox, Incubation, W3C Process, IETF RFC stream) to the artifact, the synthesizer will auto-reject; phrase the fix without those tokens.
